node.name: Baked Salmon Plate — Maple-Kissed Bake
node.short_description: Baked salmon with lemon, served with mixed vegetables and rice.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve salmon with a squeeze of lemon over a bed of rice and accompany with steamed or roasted mixed vegetables for a balanced plate.
node.nutrition_note: High-protein meal with omega-3 fats from salmon; complex carbohydrates from rice; fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables; vitamin C from lemon. Low in added sugars unless a glaze is used.

node.name: Baked Salmon Plate — Mild-Spice Skillet
node.short_description: Baked salmon with mild spices, lemon, mixed vegetables, and rice.
node.meal_suggestion: Serving idea: 4–6 oz salmon, 1 cup cooked rice, 1–2 cups mixed vegetables; drizzle with lemon juice and a touch of olive oil or herbs. For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa.
node.nutrition_note: High-protein, omega-3-rich salmon; moderate carbs from rice; fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables; lemon adds vitamin C. Overall balanced and heart-healthy; suitable for portion-controlled dinners. Use minimal added salt if using spice blends.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 3 > Dinner > Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Smoky Style

node.name: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Smoky Style
node.short_description: Smoky teriyaki chicken with vegetables and rice
node.meal_suggestion: Grill the chicken with a smoky spice rub (paprika or chipotle), serve with 1 cup cooked brown rice and 1.5–2 cups mixed vegetables; use low-sodium teriyaki or homemade to limit sodium المحتوى.
node.nutrition_note: Protein-rich chicken with carbs from rice and fiber from vegetables. Teriyaki sauce adds sugars and sodium, so consider a low-sodium or homemade sauce to reduce salt; portion control helps manage calories.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 5 > Dinner > Beef & Veggie Stew — Mild-Spice Bake

node.name: Beef & Veggie Stew — Mild-Spice Bake
node.short_description: Beef and veggie stew inspired by a mild-spice bake, featuring tender stew beef with potatoes, carrots, and celery simmered in savory broth.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve hot in bowls with a side of steamed greens or a simple salad, and optional crusty whole-grain bread. Garnish with fresh herbs (e.g., parsley) for added flavor without extra calories.
node.nutrition_note: Protein-rich beef provides essential amino acids; potatoes add energy-rich carbohydrates; carrots and celery supply fiber, vitamins, and minerals; broth contributes moisture and minerals. Sodium varies with broth type; to keep sodium lower, use a low-sodium broth and trim visible fat from the beef.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Child > Day 6 > Dinner > Tofu Veggie Curry — Citrus Fresh Wrap

node.name: Tofu Veggie Curry — Citrus Fresh Wrap
node.short_description: Tofu with mixed vegetables in curry sauce served with rice; a protein-rich, veggie-forward plant-based meal with a citrus-inspired fresh note.
node.meal_suggestion: To boost fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa. Opt for a low-sodium curry sauce and add a side of leafy greens for extra volume and micronutrients. A squeeze of lemon or lime can enhance the citrus freshness mentioned in the name.
node.nutrition_note: Protein-rich tofu provides plant-based protein; fiber from vegetables and rice supports fullness. Carbohydrates mainly come from rice. Depending on the curry sauce, sodium and added sugars may be high; check for dairy and gluten content if you have sensitivities.
